bestCloser.name: Julia Dusseau
bestCloser.insight: Julia closes at highest rate AND highest margin. She diversifies across 35 clients vs Mike's 20. She's the model to replicate.

mikeProfile.name: Mike Dades
mikeProfile.insight: Mike handles volume (2x Julia's count) but at lower margin. His deals are bigger but less profitable per hour.

insights.juliaVsMike: Julia averages $37/hr margin vs Mike's $20/hr. Julia's secret: higher-margin Non-VMS and direct hire deals. Mike runs more VMS volume. New BDMs should follow Julia's playbook.

tier1_volume.risk: High volume but lowest margin. Non-start rate 10%.

tier2_highMargin.insight: Highest margin major client. Worth aggressive expansion.
High margin + diversity angle. Priority target.
Creative agency, highest margin small client.

clientTiers.insight: Amazon drives volume but at $14/hr margin. Microsoft at $30/hr is 2x better per placement. F5 at $46/hr is the real money. Strategy: maintain Amazon volume, actively grow Microsoft/F5/Starbucks/T-Mobile.

seasonality.insight: Q3 is peak (Jul-Sep = 744). December drops 40% from October. Start prospecting in August for Q4 close. January recovers fast.

vmsInsights.insight: Non-VMS pays 47% more margin than Beeline ($28 vs $19). Fieldglass is the best VMS ($30/hr, 94% win rate). IQN has near-perfect 98% win rate.

contractDuration.insight: Average contractor stays 10 months. Plan for renewal conversations at month 8.

geography.insight: 84% of active placements are in WA. California is the next frontier. East coast (VA, NY) is tiny but growing.

factors.reason: Fast responders convert at much higher rates
Rylem's certification is a competitive moat
Deals that go cold rarely recover
